summary.text: The dominant pattern today is sustained high attention on coding agents and MCP/tool-calling projects, with Kilo Code (34.6x acceleration) and Headroom (1.5x acceleration) leading breakout signals. Kilo Code's sudden breakout suggests developer interest in multi-IDE, zero-markup AI coding agents, while Headroom's sustained growth indicates token cost optimization remains a key pain point. Agent Reach continues its multi-week hot streak in MCP, confirming the category's momentum. On Hacker News, a satirical post about SpaceX buying Cursor for $60B (1120 votes) and a report that 60% of US consumers dislike 'AI' in branding (1035 votes) reflect both hype and skepticism around AI coding tools. Cross-source confirmation is weak, with only two items appearing across GitHub and other platforms. The local LLM inference category is notably absent from top movers, suggesting attention has shifted toward agentic and tool-use layers rather than model deployment.

projects.name: ncnn
projects.description: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for the mobile platform

llmSummary.oneLiner: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for mobile, embedded, and desktop deployment, with no third-party runtime dependencies and support for CPU and Vulkan GPU backends.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Deploying deep learning models efficiently on resource-constrained devices like phones, embedded systems, and edge devices without heavy dependencies.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project shows a sudden breakout with a 7-day average star delta of 3.4 and an acceleration ratio of 6 (high), indicating a sharp increase in attention. With 23,380 stars and a recent release within 30 days, it's gaining momentum likely due to its practical utility for on-device AI deployment.
llmSummary.whatChanged: A recent release (within 30 days) and continuous commits suggest ongoing improvements. The README highlights new platform support (HarmonyOS, visionOS) and the pnnx tool for easy PyTorch/ONNX conversion, which may have attracted new users.
llmSummary.whyTrending: ncnn is trending because it offers a lightweight, dependency-free solution for running neural networks on mobile and edge devices, a critical need as AI moves to the edge. Its support for multiple backends (CPU, Vulkan) and frameworks (PyTorch, ONNX) makes it versatile.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: For developers building mobile or edge AI apps, ncnn provides a production-ready inference engine with minimal overhead and broad platform support. The pnnx tool simplifies model conversion from PyTorch/ONNX, lowering the barrier to entry.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward on-device AI inference without cloud dependencies. Developers should consider ncnn for latency-sensitive or privacy-preserving applications on Android, iOS, and embedded Linux.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Mobile app developers, embedded systems engineers, AI researchers deploying models on edge devices, and developers working with Android, iOS, or Vulkan.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has 1,183 open issues, indicating active development but also potential bugs or feature gaps. The C++ API may have a steeper learning curve for Python-only developers, though Python bindings exist.

projects.name: Agent-Reach
projects.description: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Agent Reach is a CLI tool that gives AI agents zero-config access to read and search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and more — all free, no API fees.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents lack native ability to read web content from platforms that require authentication, paid APIs, or complex scraping setups. Agent Reach solves this by providing a unified installer and channel router that selects the best free backend for each platform, handles authentication, and lets agents access content via simple commands.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo exploded from 31,787 stars with a 24h delta of +2,809 and a 7-day delta of +7,284. The acceleration ratio is 3.3 (high), indicating a sudden breakout. This is a massive spike in attention, likely driven by viral sharing in AI developer communities.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README is comprehensive and was recently updated (pushed 2026-06-16). The project has a recent release and active commits within 7 days. The breakout may be fueled by word-of-mouth among Claude Code and Cursor users who need free web access for their agents.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a universal pain point for AI agent users: getting content from walled-garden platforms without paying for APIs or writing custom scrapers. The 'one CLI, zero API fees' pitch is compelling, and the project's design (multi-backend routing, self-diagnosis, easy install) reduces friction. The Chinese-language README also taps into a large developer base in China.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: For AI agent builders, this is a ready-made capability layer that can be integrated into any agent that runs shell commands. It eliminates the need to build and maintain scrapers for multiple platforms. Developers can use it to give their agents web-reading abilities in minutes.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward 'capability layers' that abstract away the complexity of platform-specific access. Instead of each agent reinventing the wheel for web scraping, a shared open-source layer can handle authentication, backend selection, and fallback routing. This pattern may become standard for agent infrastructure.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI agent developers and power users who use Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw, Windsurf, or similar tools and need their agents to read content from Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and other platforms without paying for APIs.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on third-party tools and browser cookies for some platforms (Twitter, Reddit, Xiaohongshu), which carries account ban risk if detected. Some backends may break when platforms update their anti-scraping measures. The project's long-term maintenance depends on the author's continued engagement.

projects.name: headroom
projects.description: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Headroom compresses tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks by 60-95% before they reach the LLM, preserving answer quality while slashing token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents waste massive token budgets on verbose tool outputs, logs, and context, driving up cost and latency. Headroom reduces token consumption by 60-95% without degrading answer quality, using local, reversible compression.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 32,126 stars and a 7-day average of 3,893 stars/day, Headroom is one of the fastest-growing AI infrastructure projects. The 24-hour delta of 1,892 stars and acceleration of 1.5 indicate sustained, high-velocity organic growth. The project has a recent release and active commits, signaling ongoing development and community trust.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and continuous commits suggest steady improvement, and the README mentions new features like output token reduction and Copilot CLI subscription mode, which may have driven recent attention.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Headroom addresses a universal pain point for AI agent users: token cost. By offering multiple integration modes (library, proxy, MCP, agent wrap) and proven benchmarks showing 92% savings on real workloads, it appeals to both individual developers and enterprises. The reversible compression (CCR) and cross-agent memory features add unique value.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Headroom is a practical, drop-in solution to reduce LLM costs for any AI agent workflow. It supports Python and TypeScript, works with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, and more, and requires minimal setup.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Token optimization is becoming a critical layer in the AI stack. Headroom's approach — local, reversible, multi-algorithm compression — signals a shift toward treating context as a first-class resource that needs active management, not just passive consumption.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers and teams using coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, Copilot CLI) or building custom LLM applications who want to reduce token costs without changing their workflow.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new (32k stars but only 2k forks) and has 287 open issues, which may indicate growing pains. Compression quality may vary by content type, and the output token reduction feature is still experimental. Dependency on local compute for compression could be a bottleneck for some users.

projects.name: kilocode
projects.description: Kilo is the all-in-one agentic engineering platform. Build, ship, and iterate faster with the most popular open source coding agent.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Kilo Code is an open-source AI coding agent that works across VS Code, JetBrains, and CLI, offering 500+ models with zero markup pricing.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers need a unified, cost-transparent AI coding assistant that works across multiple IDEs and the terminal, with the flexibility to switch models mid-task and avoid vendor lock-in.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo gained 1,165 stars in the last 24 hours and 1,333 in the last 7 days, with a high acceleration of 34.6 (well above the 2.0 threshold). Total stars are 21,326, indicating a sudden breakout in attention. The three-day average star delta (33.7) and seven-day average (27.9) show sustained high velocity, but the acceleration spike suggests a recent catalyst.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent release (within 30 days) and active commits (within 7 days) indicate ongoing development that may have attracted attention. The README mentions support for GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.7, and other cutting-edge models, which could be a draw.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Kilo Code offers a compelling value proposition: open-source, multi-platform (VS Code, JetBrains, CLI), 500+ models with zero markup, and specialized agents for code, plan, ask, debug, and review. The combination of flexibility, cost transparency, and broad IDE support resonates with developers seeking an alternative to proprietary AI coding tools.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Kilo Code is a strong contender in the AI coding agent space, especially for teams that want to avoid vendor lock-in and control model costs. Its multi-platform support and mid-task model switching are differentiators.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward open-source, model-agnostic coding agents that prioritize developer choice and cost efficiency. The ability to switch models mid-task could become a standard expectation, and the MCP marketplace integration hints at an extensible ecosystem.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and engineering teams using VS Code, JetBrains, or CLI who want an AI coding assistant with flexible model selection, transparent pricing, and the ability to run autonomously in CI/CD pipelines.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is a fork of OpenCode, which may raise questions about long-term maintenance and differentiation. With 789 open issues, the project may face scalability challenges as it grows. The sudden breakout could attract scrutiny and competition from established players.

projects.name: open-design
projects.description: 🎨 Local-first, open-source Claude Design alternative. 🖥️ Native desktop app. ⚡ 259+ Skills · ✨ 142+ Design Systems 🖼️ Web · desktop · mobile prototypes · slides · images · videos · HyperFrames 📦 Sandboxed preview · HTML/PDF/PPTX/MP4 export 🤖 Claude Code / OpenClaw / Codex / Cursor / OpenCode / Qwen / Copilot / Hermes / Kimi & 17+ CLIs.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Open Design is a local-first, open-source desktop app that turns your coding agent into a design engine, generating prototypes, slides, images, and videos from a single design system.
llmSummary.problemSolved: It replaces closed, cloud-dependent design tools (like Claude Design or Figma) with an agent-native, filesystem-based workspace where design systems, skills, and plugins are local files that any coding agent can read, write, and remix. It solves the lock-in and latency of proprietary design tools by making the design process fully local, scriptable, and agent-driven.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 66,964 stars, a 24-hour delta of +844, and a 7-day average delta of 532.9, this project is sustaining explosive growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.5 indicates high but steady momentum, not a sudden spike. The recent 0.10.0 release (all-in-one agentic workspace) and the launch of the AMR model router likely fuel continued adoption. The project is clearly resonating with the developer community as a practical, open alternative to Claude Design.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The 0.10.0 release introduced an all-in-one agentic design workspace with parallel sessions, making the tool feel like a local design team. Additionally, the launch of the AMR (Agentic Model Router) service provides a unified billing and model access layer for GPT, Claude, Gemini, and DeepSeek. These product milestones, combined with active community building (Fellow program, Discord), are driving the sustained growth.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It taps into the zeitgeist of 'vibe coding' and agent-native workflows, offering a local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design and Figma. The ability to use any coding agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) as the design engine, combined with 150+ design systems and 260+ plugins, makes it a powerful and flexible tool for developers who want to keep their design process in their own environment.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Open Design demonstrates that the future of design tools is agent-native and local-first. Developers should consider how their own tools can be reimagined as filesystem-based, CLI-friendly, and MCP-compatible to integrate seamlessly with the growing ecosystem of coding agents.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a major workflow shift: design is becoming a programmable, agent-driven activity. For AI developers, the opportunity lies in building plugins, skills, and integrations that extend Open Design's capabilities. The project's architecture (MCP server, BYOK proxy, design systems as DESIGN.md files) provides a blueprint for creating composable, agent-friendly tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and designers who want to generate UI prototypes, slides, images, and videos using AI coding agents, while keeping their design systems and data local. Also, teams looking for an open-source alternative to Claude Design or Figma that integrates with their existing agent workflows.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is still evolving rapidly (413 open issues) and may have breaking changes. The reliance on external coding agents means the quality of output depends on the agent's capabilities. The 'local-first' approach may limit collaboration features compared to cloud-based tools. Additionally, the project's license (Apache 2.0) is permissive, but the AMR service is a paid offering, which could create a split between free and paid features.

projects.name: agent-skills
projects.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A production-grade skill pack for AI coding agents that encodes senior engineer workflows, quality gates, and best practices into reusable Markdown files.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents often produce inconsistent, low-quality code because they lack the structured workflows, verification gates, and engineering discipline that senior developers apply. Agent Skills packages these practices into slash commands and skill files that agents can follow automatically.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 61,464 stars, a 24-hour delta of +1,171, and a 7-day average of +1,823 stars per day, this project is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.7 (moderate but positive) indicates continued momentum without a sudden spike. It has become a go-to resource for developers integrating AI agents into their workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and continuous commits suggest ongoing refinement, and the broad compatibility with Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, and other tools has likely driven steady adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Agent Skills addresses a critical pain point: making AI agents produce production-quality code. Its practical, tool-agnostic approach (plain Markdown skills) and endorsement by Addy Osmani (a well-known engineering leader) have fueled rapid adoption across the developer community.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a must-try for any developer using AI coding agents. It provides a ready-made set of workflows that can be dropped into Claude Code, Cursor, or other agents to immediately improve code quality and consistency.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of structured skill packs signals a shift from 'prompt engineering' to 'workflow engineering' for AI agents. Developers should expect more curated, domain-specific skill libraries to emerge, making agent behavior more predictable and reliable.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, etc.) who want to enforce consistent engineering practices and improve code quality.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is heavily dependent on the ecosystem of AI coding tools; changes in those tools' plugin systems could break compatibility. Also, the skills are opinionated — teams with different workflows may need to adapt them.

projects.name: DeepSeek-Reasonix
projects.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go for performance and prefix-cache stability.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Provides a configurable, plugin-driven AI coding assistant that runs as a single static Go binary, optimized for DeepSeek's prefix cache to reduce token costs during long sessions.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 23,001 stars, a 24-hour delta of +260, and a 7-day delta of +2,152, this project shows sustained high velocity. The acceleration ratio of 0.8 indicates steady growth without sudden spikes, suggesting consistent community interest and adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The recent 1.0 ground-up rewrite in Go (from TypeScript) and the release of a new binary distribution likely drove attention. The README highlights a migration guide and new features, and the project has a recent release within 30 days.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It taps into the demand for efficient, DeepSeek-optimized coding agents that minimize API costs through prefix caching. The Go rewrite promises better performance and cross-platform distribution, appealing to developers seeking lightweight, configurable AI tools.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: A practical tool for developers who want a terminal-based AI coding assistant with low token overhead, especially those using DeepSeek models. Its plugin system and config-driven design make it extensible.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Signals a shift toward specialized, model-native agents that optimize for specific LLM features (like prefix caching). The Go rewrite indicates a preference for performance and single-binary distribution over JavaScript ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using DeepSeek models who want a terminal-based AI coding agent; users of CLI tools and AI agents; those seeking a configurable, plugin-driven assistant with low token costs.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix-caching behavior; if DeepSeek changes pricing or caching, the value proposition weakens. The project is still evolving (661 open issues), and the Go rewrite may introduce breaking changes for legacy users.

projects.name: code-review-graph
projects.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Local-first code intelligence graph that slashes AI token usage by ~82x median during code review by providing precise, incremental context via MCP.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding tools waste tokens re-reading entire codebases during reviews; this builds a persistent, incremental AST graph so AI reads only the blast radius of changes.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 18,484 stars, a 7-day average delta of 50.4 stars/day, and acceleration of 0.7 (high sustained velocity), this project is consistently gaining attention. The 24h delta of 38 stars and 7d delta of 341 stars show strong, steady growth without sudden spikes, indicating deep developer interest in token-efficient AI workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits and a release within 30 days suggest active maintenance, and the README benchmarks (528x max token reduction) likely continue to drive word-of-mouth.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It directly addresses the pain point of token costs and context limits in AI-assisted coding, offering a practical, local-first solution that integrates with 13+ AI tools via MCP. The benchmarked results (82x median reduction) provide compelling evidence.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use AI coding assistants on large or monorepo projects, this tool can dramatically reduce token consumption and improve review accuracy by feeding only relevant context.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward local, graph-based context management for AI tools, reducing reliance on expensive LLM context windows and enabling more efficient, privacy-preserving code review workflows.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) on large codebases or monorepos who want to reduce token costs and improve review quality.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Requires Python 3.10+ and Tree-sitter; may have limited language support for niche languages without custom config. The 133 open issues suggest active community but also potential rough edges.

projects.name: ai-engineering-from-scratch
projects.description: Learn it. Build it. Ship it for others.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production agents, with every lesson producing a reusable artifact.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Most AI learning resources are scattered, shallow, or API-focused. This curriculum provides a structured, end-to-end path that builds deep understanding by implementing algorithms from scratch before using frameworks, and ensures every lesson produces a practical, shippable artifact.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 32,318 stars, a 7-day average delta of 405 stars/day, and a 24-hour delta of 605 stars, this repo is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.9 indicates strong and consistent momentum, not a sudden spike. The project has attracted over 150,000 readers and 241,000 page views in the last 30 days, signaling broad community adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README was recently updated (pushed June 14, 2026) and the curriculum continues to expand, but no major release or external event is evident.
llmSummary.whyTrending: The curriculum fills a clear gap: it offers a free, comprehensive, hands-on path from math to production agents, with a unique 'build it, use it, ship it' philosophy. The inclusion of multiple languages (Python, TypeScript, Rust, Julia) and the focus on producing reusable artifacts (prompts, skills, agents, MCP servers) resonates with developers who want deep understanding and practical output.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a high-quality, structured resource for anyone serious about AI engineering. It's worth cloning and exploring, especially for developers who want to move beyond API wrappers and understand the underlying mechanics.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The project signals a growing demand for deep, foundational AI education that prioritizes understanding over speed. Developers are increasingly seeking resources that teach them to build from scratch, not just consume frameworks. The curriculum's emphasis on shipping artifacts also reflects a shift toward practical, portfolio-building learning.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and engineers who want to learn AI engineering from the ground up, from math to production. Suitable for beginners with coding experience and experienced practitioners looking to fill gaps in their understanding.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The curriculum is massive (503 lessons, ~320 hours) and may be overwhelming. Some lessons may be incomplete or in progress. The project is maintained by a single creator, so long-term sustainability depends on continued contributions and community support.

watchlistMovements.name: Agent-Reach
watchlistMovements.description: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Agent Reach is a CLI tool that gives AI agents zero-config access to read and search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and more — all free, no API fees.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents lack native ability to read web content from platforms that require authentication, paid APIs, or complex scraping setups. Agent Reach solves this by providing a unified installer and channel router that selects the best free backend for each platform, handles authentication, and lets agents access content via simple commands.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo exploded from 31,787 stars with a 24h delta of +2,809 and a 7-day delta of +7,284. The acceleration ratio is 3.3 (high), indicating a sudden breakout. This is a massive spike in attention, likely driven by viral sharing in AI developer communities.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README is comprehensive and was recently updated (pushed 2026-06-16). The project has a recent release and active commits within 7 days. The breakout may be fueled by word-of-mouth among Claude Code and Cursor users who need free web access for their agents.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a universal pain point for AI agent users: getting content from walled-garden platforms without paying for APIs or writing custom scrapers. The 'one CLI, zero API fees' pitch is compelling, and the project's design (multi-backend routing, self-diagnosis, easy install) reduces friction. The Chinese-language README also taps into a large developer base in China.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: For AI agent builders, this is a ready-made capability layer that can be integrated into any agent that runs shell commands. It eliminates the need to build and maintain scrapers for multiple platforms. Developers can use it to give their agents web-reading abilities in minutes.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward 'capability layers' that abstract away the complexity of platform-specific access. Instead of each agent reinventing the wheel for web scraping, a shared open-source layer can handle authentication, backend selection, and fallback routing. This pattern may become standard for agent infrastructure.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI agent developers and power users who use Claude Code, Cursor, OpenClaw, Windsurf, or similar tools and need their agents to read content from Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, and other platforms without paying for APIs.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on third-party tools and browser cookies for some platforms (Twitter, Reddit, Xiaohongshu), which carries account ban risk if detected. Some backends may break when platforms update their anti-scraping measures. The project's long-term maintenance depends on the author's continued engagement.

watchlistMovements.name: headroom
watchlistMovements.description: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Headroom compresses tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks by 60-95% before they reach the LLM, preserving answer quality while slashing token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents waste massive token budgets on verbose tool outputs, logs, and context, driving up cost and latency. Headroom reduces token consumption by 60-95% without degrading answer quality, using local, reversible compression.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 32,126 stars and a 7-day average of 3,893 stars/day, Headroom is one of the fastest-growing AI infrastructure projects. The 24-hour delta of 1,892 stars and acceleration of 1.5 indicate sustained, high-velocity organic growth. The project has a recent release and active commits, signaling ongoing development and community trust.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and continuous commits suggest steady improvement, and the README mentions new features like output token reduction and Copilot CLI subscription mode, which may have driven recent attention.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Headroom addresses a universal pain point for AI agent users: token cost. By offering multiple integration modes (library, proxy, MCP, agent wrap) and proven benchmarks showing 92% savings on real workloads, it appeals to both individual developers and enterprises. The reversible compression (CCR) and cross-agent memory features add unique value.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Headroom is a practical, drop-in solution to reduce LLM costs for any AI agent workflow. It supports Python and TypeScript, works with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, and more, and requires minimal setup.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Token optimization is becoming a critical layer in the AI stack. Headroom's approach — local, reversible, multi-algorithm compression — signals a shift toward treating context as a first-class resource that needs active management, not just passive consumption.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers and teams using coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Aider, Copilot CLI) or building custom LLM applications who want to reduce token costs without changing their workflow.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new (32k stars but only 2k forks) and has 287 open issues, which may indicate growing pains. Compression quality may vary by content type, and the output token reduction feature is still experimental. Dependency on local compute for compression could be a bottleneck for some users.

watchlistMovements.name: kilocode
watchlistMovements.description: Kilo is the all-in-one agentic engineering platform. Build, ship, and iterate faster with the most popular open source coding agent.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Kilo Code is an open-source AI coding agent that works across VS Code, JetBrains, and CLI, offering 500+ models with zero markup pricing.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Developers need a unified, cost-transparent AI coding assistant that works across multiple IDEs and the terminal, with the flexibility to switch models mid-task and avoid vendor lock-in.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo gained 1,165 stars in the last 24 hours and 1,333 in the last 7 days, with a high acceleration of 34.6 (well above the 2.0 threshold). Total stars are 21,326, indicating a sudden breakout in attention. The three-day average star delta (33.7) and seven-day average (27.9) show sustained high velocity, but the acceleration spike suggests a recent catalyst.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent release (within 30 days) and active commits (within 7 days) indicate ongoing development that may have attracted attention. The README mentions support for GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.7, and other cutting-edge models, which could be a draw.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Kilo Code offers a compelling value proposition: open-source, multi-platform (VS Code, JetBrains, CLI), 500+ models with zero markup, and specialized agents for code, plan, ask, debug, and review. The combination of flexibility, cost transparency, and broad IDE support resonates with developers seeking an alternative to proprietary AI coding tools.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Kilo Code is a strong contender in the AI coding agent space, especially for teams that want to avoid vendor lock-in and control model costs. Its multi-platform support and mid-task model switching are differentiators.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward open-source, model-agnostic coding agents that prioritize developer choice and cost efficiency. The ability to switch models mid-task could become a standard expectation, and the MCP marketplace integration hints at an extensible ecosystem.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and engineering teams using VS Code, JetBrains, or CLI who want an AI coding assistant with flexible model selection, transparent pricing, and the ability to run autonomously in CI/CD pipelines.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is a fork of OpenCode, which may raise questions about long-term maintenance and differentiation. With 789 open issues, the project may face scalability challenges as it grows. The sudden breakout could attract scrutiny and competition from established players.

watchlistMovements.name: open-design
watchlistMovements.description: 🎨 Local-first, open-source Claude Design alternative. 🖥️ Native desktop app. ⚡ 259+ Skills · ✨ 142+ Design Systems 🖼️ Web · desktop · mobile prototypes · slides · images · videos · HyperFrames 📦 Sandboxed preview · HTML/PDF/PPTX/MP4 export 🤖 Claude Code / OpenClaw / Codex / Cursor / OpenCode / Qwen / Copilot / Hermes / Kimi & 17+ CLIs.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Open Design is a local-first, open-source desktop app that turns your coding agent into a design engine, generating prototypes, slides, images, and videos from a single design system.
llmSummary.problemSolved: It replaces closed, cloud-dependent design tools (like Claude Design or Figma) with an agent-native, filesystem-based workspace where design systems, skills, and plugins are local files that any coding agent can read, write, and remix. It solves the lock-in and latency of proprietary design tools by making the design process fully local, scriptable, and agent-driven.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 66,964 stars, a 24-hour delta of +844, and a 7-day average delta of 532.9, this project is sustaining explosive growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.5 indicates high but steady momentum, not a sudden spike. The recent 0.10.0 release (all-in-one agentic workspace) and the launch of the AMR model router likely fuel continued adoption. The project is clearly resonating with the developer community as a practical, open alternative to Claude Design.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The 0.10.0 release introduced an all-in-one agentic design workspace with parallel sessions, making the tool feel like a local design team. Additionally, the launch of the AMR (Agentic Model Router) service provides a unified billing and model access layer for GPT, Claude, Gemini, and DeepSeek. These product milestones, combined with active community building (Fellow program, Discord), are driving the sustained growth.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It taps into the zeitgeist of 'vibe coding' and agent-native workflows, offering a local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design and Figma. The ability to use any coding agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) as the design engine, combined with 150+ design systems and 260+ plugins, makes it a powerful and flexible tool for developers who want to keep their design process in their own environment.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Open Design demonstrates that the future of design tools is agent-native and local-first. Developers should consider how their own tools can be reimagined as filesystem-based, CLI-friendly, and MCP-compatible to integrate seamlessly with the growing ecosystem of coding agents.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a major workflow shift: design is becoming a programmable, agent-driven activity. For AI developers, the opportunity lies in building plugins, skills, and integrations that extend Open Design's capabilities. The project's architecture (MCP server, BYOK proxy, design systems as DESIGN.md files) provides a blueprint for creating composable, agent-friendly tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and designers who want to generate UI prototypes, slides, images, and videos using AI coding agents, while keeping their design systems and data local. Also, teams looking for an open-source alternative to Claude Design or Figma that integrates with their existing agent workflows.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is still evolving rapidly (413 open issues) and may have breaking changes. The reliance on external coding agents means the quality of output depends on the agent's capabilities. The 'local-first' approach may limit collaboration features compared to cloud-based tools. Additionally, the project's license (Apache 2.0) is permissive, but the AMR service is a paid offering, which could create a split between free and paid features.

watchlistMovements.name: agent-skills

llmSummary.oneLiner: A production-grade skill pack for AI coding agents that encodes senior engineer workflows, quality gates, and best practices into reusable Markdown files.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding agents often produce inconsistent, low-quality code because they lack the structured workflows, verification gates, and engineering discipline that senior developers apply. Agent Skills packages these practices into slash commands and skill files that agents can follow automatically.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 61,464 stars, a 24-hour delta of +1,171, and a 7-day average of +1,823 stars per day, this project is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.7 (moderate but positive) indicates continued momentum without a sudden spike. It has become a go-to resource for developers integrating AI agents into their workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and continuous commits suggest ongoing refinement, and the broad compatibility with Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, and other tools has likely driven steady adoption.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Agent Skills addresses a critical pain point: making AI agents produce production-quality code. Its practical, tool-agnostic approach (plain Markdown skills) and endorsement by Addy Osmani (a well-known engineering leader) have fueled rapid adoption across the developer community.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a must-try for any developer using AI coding agents. It provides a ready-made set of workflows that can be dropped into Claude Code, Cursor, or other agents to immediately improve code quality and consistency.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rise of structured skill packs signals a shift from 'prompt engineering' to 'workflow engineering' for AI agents. Developers should expect more curated, domain-specific skill libraries to emerge, making agent behavior more predictable and reliable.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and teams using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, etc.) who want to enforce consistent engineering practices and improve code quality.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is heavily dependent on the ecosystem of AI coding tools; changes in those tools' plugin systems could break compatibility. Also, the skills are opinionated — teams with different workflows may need to adapt them.

watchlistMovements.name: DeepSeek-Reasonix
watchlistMovements.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go for performance and prefix-cache stability.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Provides a configurable, plugin-driven AI coding assistant that runs as a single static Go binary, optimized for DeepSeek's prefix cache to reduce token costs during long sessions.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 23,001 stars, a 24-hour delta of +260, and a 7-day delta of +2,152, this project shows sustained high velocity. The acceleration ratio of 0.8 indicates steady growth without sudden spikes, suggesting consistent community interest and adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The recent 1.0 ground-up rewrite in Go (from TypeScript) and the release of a new binary distribution likely drove attention. The README highlights a migration guide and new features, and the project has a recent release within 30 days.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It taps into the demand for efficient, DeepSeek-optimized coding agents that minimize API costs through prefix caching. The Go rewrite promises better performance and cross-platform distribution, appealing to developers seeking lightweight, configurable AI tools.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: A practical tool for developers who want a terminal-based AI coding assistant with low token overhead, especially those using DeepSeek models. Its plugin system and config-driven design make it extensible.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Signals a shift toward specialized, model-native agents that optimize for specific LLM features (like prefix caching). The Go rewrite indicates a preference for performance and single-binary distribution over JavaScript ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using DeepSeek models who want a terminal-based AI coding agent; users of CLI tools and AI agents; those seeking a configurable, plugin-driven assistant with low token costs.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix-caching behavior; if DeepSeek changes pricing or caching, the value proposition weakens. The project is still evolving (661 open issues), and the Go rewrite may introduce breaking changes for legacy users.

watchlistMovements.name: code-review-graph
watchlistMovements.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Local-first code intelligence graph that slashes AI token usage by ~82x median during code review by providing precise, incremental context via MCP.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding tools waste tokens re-reading entire codebases during reviews; this builds a persistent, incremental AST graph so AI reads only the blast radius of changes.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 18,484 stars, a 7-day average delta of 50.4 stars/day, and acceleration of 0.7 (high sustained velocity), this project is consistently gaining attention. The 24h delta of 38 stars and 7d delta of 341 stars show strong, steady growth without sudden spikes, indicating deep developer interest in token-efficient AI workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits and a release within 30 days suggest active maintenance, and the README benchmarks (528x max token reduction) likely continue to drive word-of-mouth.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It directly addresses the pain point of token costs and context limits in AI-assisted coding, offering a practical, local-first solution that integrates with 13+ AI tools via MCP. The benchmarked results (82x median reduction) provide compelling evidence.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you use AI coding assistants on large or monorepo projects, this tool can dramatically reduce token consumption and improve review accuracy by feeding only relevant context.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward local, graph-based context management for AI tools, reducing reliance on expensive LLM context windows and enabling more efficient, privacy-preserving code review workflows.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) on large codebases or monorepos who want to reduce token costs and improve review quality.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Requires Python 3.10+ and Tree-sitter; may have limited language support for niche languages without custom config. The 133 open issues suggest active community but also potential rough edges.

llmSummary.oneLiner: An ultra-lightweight, open-source personal AI agent that you can truly own, with WebUI, multi-channel chat, tools, memory, MCP, and model routing.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Most AI agents are either too heavy, tied to a single provider, or not truly controllable by the user. nanobot provides a small, readable core that integrates with multiple LLM providers, chat platforms, and tools, giving users a personal agent they can run and customize themselves.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 44,391 stars, a 24-hour star delta of +161, and a 7-day average of +46.3, the project is experiencing a sudden breakout (acceleration 4.6). The high velocity and recent release (v0.2.1 on 2026-06-01) signal strong community interest and rapid adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The project released v0.2.1 'The Workbench Release' on 2026-06-01, which turned the WebUI into a daily agent workbench with clearer timelines, live file-edit activity, project workspaces, and broader provider/channel support. This major feature release likely triggered the surge.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It offers a practical, lightweight alternative to heavy agent frameworks, supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) and chat channels (Telegram, Discord, WeChat, etc.), and is actively developed with daily releases and a responsive community.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: nanobot is a strong candidate for developers who want to build custom AI agents without vendor lock-in. Its modular architecture and support for MCP, tools, and memory make it extensible for production workflows.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The trend toward lightweight, multi-provider, user-owned AI agents is accelerating. nanobot's rapid adoption suggests developers are seeking alternatives to monolithic, provider-specific solutions. This signals an opportunity for building composable agent ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and power users who want a personal AI agent that integrates with their existing tools and chat platforms, and who value control, privacy, and the ability to customize or extend the agent.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is very young (first release ~April 2026) and has a high number of open issues (877) relative to its size, which may indicate growing pains or rapid feature churn. The sudden breakout could be hype-driven; long-term sustainability is unproven.

watchlistMovements.description: Learn it. Build it. Ship it for others.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production agents, with every lesson producing a reusable artifact.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Most AI learning resources are scattered, shallow, or API-focused. This curriculum provides a structured, end-to-end path that builds deep understanding by implementing algorithms from scratch before using frameworks, and ensures every lesson produces a practical, shippable artifact.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 32,318 stars, a 7-day average delta of 405 stars/day, and a 24-hour delta of 605 stars, this repo is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.9 indicates strong and consistent momentum, not a sudden spike. The project has attracted over 150,000 readers and 241,000 page views in the last 30 days, signaling broad community adoption.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The README was recently updated (pushed June 14, 2026) and the curriculum continues to expand, but no major release or external event is evident.
llmSummary.whyTrending: The curriculum fills a clear gap: it offers a free, comprehensive, hands-on path from math to production agents, with a unique 'build it, use it, ship it' philosophy. The inclusion of multiple languages (Python, TypeScript, Rust, Julia) and the focus on producing reusable artifacts (prompts, skills, agents, MCP servers) resonates with developers who want deep understanding and practical output.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This is a high-quality, structured resource for anyone serious about AI engineering. It's worth cloning and exploring, especially for developers who want to move beyond API wrappers and understand the underlying mechanics.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The project signals a growing demand for deep, foundational AI education that prioritizes understanding over speed. Developers are increasingly seeking resources that teach them to build from scratch, not just consume frameworks. The curriculum's emphasis on shipping artifacts also reflects a shift toward practical, portfolio-building learning.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and engineers who want to learn AI engineering from the ground up, from math to production. Suitable for beginners with coding experience and experienced practitioners looking to fill gaps in their understanding.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The curriculum is massive (503 lessons, ~320 hours) and may be overwhelming. Some lessons may be incomplete or in progress. The project is maintained by a single creator, so long-term sustainability depends on continued contributions and community support.

modelDemoSignals.title: Native Active Perception as Reasoning for Omni-Modal Understanding
modelDemoSignals.description: Passive models for long video understanding typically rely on a "watch-it-all" paradigm, processing frames uniformly regardless of query difficulty, causing computational cost to grow with video duration. Although interactive frameworks have emerged, they often rely on global pre-scanning, and their context cost still scales with video length. modelDemoSignals.summary: Passive models for long video understanding typically rely on a "watch-it-all" paradigm, processing frames uniformly regardless of query difficulty, causing computational cost to grow with video duration. Although interactive frameworks have emerged, they often rely on global pre-scanning, and their context cost still scales with video length. We propose OmniAgent, the first native omni-modal agent that formulates video understanding as a POMDP-based iterative Observation-Thought-Action cycle. OmniAgent executes on-demand actions to selectively distill audio-visual cues into a persistent textual memory, effectively decoupling reasoning complexity from raw video duration. To operationalize this, we introduce (1) Agentic Supervised Fine-Tuning to bootstrap native active perception via best-of-N trajectory synthesis with dual-stage quality control, and (2) Agentic Reinforcement Learning with TAURA (Turn-aware Adaptive Uncertainty Rescaled Advantage), which leverages turn-level entropy to steer credit assignment toward pivotal discovery turns. Crucially, OmniAgent exhibits positive test-time scaling, where performance improves as the number of reasoning turns increases, validating the efficacy of active perception. Empirical results across ten benchmarks (e.g., VideoMME, LVBench) demonstrate that OmniAgent achieves state-of-the-art performance among open-source models. Notably, on LVBench, our 7B agent outperforms the 10$\times$ larger Qwen2.5-VL-72B (50.5% vs. 47.3%).
modelDemoSignals.author: Zhenghao Xing, Ruiyang Xu, Yuxuan Wang, Jinzheng He, Ziyang Ma, Qize Yang, Yunfei Chu, Jin Xu, Junyang Lin, Chi-Wing Fu, Pheng-Ann Heng
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, rag, fine-tuning, benchmark, agentic

modelDemoSignals.title: Learning User Simulators with Turing Rewards
modelDemoSignals.description: Learning to simulate human users in interactive settings could advance the training of agent assistants, evaluation of personalization systems, research in the social sciences, and more. Existing approaches generally do so by training a large language model (LLM) to match a single ground truth response, either by maximizing the log probability or by using a similarity reward. We instead propose {Turing-RL}: a Turing-Test-based reinforcement learning approach for training user simulator models. {
modelDemoSignals.summary: Learning to simulate human users in interactive settings could advance the training of agent assistants, evaluation of personalization systems, research in the social sciences, and more. Existing approaches generally do so by training a large language model (LLM) to match a single ground truth response, either by maximizing the log probability or by using a similarity reward. We instead propose {Turing-RL}: a Turing-Test-based reinforcement learning approach for training user simulator models. {Turing-RL} uses a discriminative Turing reward with an LLM judge to score how indistinguishable a generated response is from the real user's given the user's history, and the user simulator LLM learns to produce responses indistinguishable from what the user could have said with such rewards. Across two different domains--conversational chat and Reddit forum discussion--we find that {Turing-RL} consistently outperforms baseline methods on both LLM and human evaluation metrics. Our study suggests that optimizing for indistinguishability, rather than response matching, is effective for learning user simulators.
modelDemoSignals.author: Yingshan Susan Wang, Cedegao E. Zhang, Linlu Qiu, Zexue He, Pengyuan Li, Alex Pentland, Roger P. Levy, Yoon Kim
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, agent, evaluation

modelDemoSignals.description: Progress in legal AI increasingly depends on access to authoritative legal text at scale. Yet one of the most consequential layers of American law remains largely absent from existing machine-readable corpora: local ordinances. Local codes govern zoning, housing, business licensing, public health, noise, animal control, and many other domains of everyday regulation, but they are fragmented across vendor platforms designed for human browsing rather than bulk research access. modelDemoSignals.summary: Progress in legal AI increasingly depends on access to authoritative legal text at scale. Yet one of the most consequential layers of American law remains largely absent from existing machine-readable corpora: local ordinances. Local codes govern zoning, housing, business licensing, public health, noise, animal control, and many other domains of everyday regulation, but they are fragmented across vendor platforms designed for human browsing rather than bulk research access. We introduce LOCUS - the Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States - a comprehensive corpus and county-harmonized access layer for U.S. municipal and county ordinance codes. The raw corpus, available for release to researchers, represents nearly all publicly available municipal and county ordinance codes. The resulting raw corpus contains codes from 9,239 cities and counties. A smaller county-harmonized LOCUS access layer provides coverage for the largest 2,309 of 3,144 U.S. counties, accounting for a majority of the population. We use OCR to handle the myriad of document formats that have kept the law from being a public resource. We release the corpus with coverage metadata to support reproducibility, downstream legal AI research, and the incremental expansion of machine-readable access to local law. We train a collection of ModernBERT-based classifiers and scorers to facilitate analyzing U.S. local law among several dimensions, such as opacity and paternalism, that have not previously been studied at this scale. LOCUS-v1 and its derivative models are available at: https://huggingface.co/datasets/LocalLaws/LOCUS-v1
modelDemoSignals.author: Denis Peskoff, Joe Barrow, Christopher Vu, Diag Davenport

modelDemoSignals.title: UBP2: Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning for Efficient Preference-based Reinforcement Learning
modelDemoSignals.description: Preference-based RL provides an approach to learning reward models from pairwise comparisons of behaviors, bypassing the need for explicit reward design. However, existing methods typically rely on passive data collection and suffer from poor sample efficiency, especially during the early stages of learning. We introduce a model-based approach that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Our method, Uncertainty-Balanced P
modelDemoSignals.summary: Preference-based RL provides an approach to learning reward models from pairwise comparisons of behaviors, bypassing the need for explicit reward design. However, existing methods typically rely on passive data collection and suffer from poor sample efficiency, especially during the early stages of learning. We introduce a model-based approach that actively directs exploration by jointly reasoning over uncertainties in the reward, dynamics, and value functions. Our method, Uncertainty-Balanced Preference Planning (UBP2), uses ensembles of reward, dynamics, and value function models to evaluate candidate trajectories according to a unified score that combines expected reward, terminal value, and epistemic uncertainty. Planning under this objective yields an explicit tradeoff between exploitation and information acquisition without requiring ad hoc exploration heuristics. Under standard regularity assumptions, we establish sublinear regret guarantees for both finite-horizon and infinite-horizon settings. Empirically, experiments on the Meta-World benchmark show UBP2 achieves substantially higher sample efficiency than model-free preference-based methods and non-optimistic model-based baselines.
modelDemoSignals.author: Mohamed Nabail, Leo Cheng, Jingmin Wang, Nicholas Rhinehart

modelDemoSignals.title: Rethinking Reward Supervision: Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation
modelDemoSignals.description: Post-training of reasoning language models is commonly driven by supervised distillation and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. Distillation often relies on chain-of-thought annotations that are expensive to obtain and may themselves be noisy, incomplete, or partially incorrect; even when the final solution is correct, an imperfect rationale can interfere with learning. Reinforcement learning with verified rewards, on the other hand, typically compresses evaluative feedback into a s
modelDemoSignals.summary: Post-training of reasoning language models is commonly driven by supervised distillation and reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards. Distillation often relies on chain-of-thought annotations that are expensive to obtain and may themselves be noisy, incomplete, or partially incorrect; even when the final solution is correct, an imperfect rationale can interfere with learning. Reinforcement learning with verified rewards, on the other hand, typically compresses evaluative feedback into a scalar signal, obscuring which aspects of a response should be improved. We propose \textbf{Rubric-Conditioned Self-Distillation}, a framework that incorporates rubrics as structured, fine-grained feedback for on-policy self-distillation. Our method conditions the teacher model on criterion-level rubrics and uses it to provide token-level guidance on the student's own sampled trajectories. This design avoids treating a single reference rationale as the sole supervision target. Instead, rubrics specify what a strong response should satisfy, enabling more fine-grained credit assignment over the reasoning process than scalar reward optimization. We instantiate this framework with a two-stage pipeline that first learns to generate task-specific rubrics and then trains a rubric-guided reasoner. We evaluate on a diverse suite of science reasoning benchmarks and results show that rubric-conditioned self-distillation effectively converts rubric-level criteria into token-level guidance over the reasoning process, surpassing GRPO by 1.0 points and OPSD by 0.9 points on average.
modelDemoSignals.author: Siyi Gu, Jialin Chen, Sophia Zhou, Arman Cohan, Rex Ying
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, benchmark

aihotHighlights.title: MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 在 SGLang-Omni 上：原生流式 48 kHz 语音服务
aihotHighlights.summary: MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 是一款开源 TTS 模型，支持 48 kHz 立体声、零样本声音克隆、最长 10 分钟长文本合成、时长控制及 31 种语言。其核心采用 Qwen3-4B 骨干与约 2B 参数的 MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2 音频编解码器，通过 12 个 RVQ 码本运行。SGLang-Omni 以三阶段流水线部署该模型。在 Seed-TTS-Eval 上词错误率 5.10%、语音相似度 69.23%，CV3-Eval 上 WER 7.48%、SIM 61.59%，MiniMax Multilingual 上 WER 6.37%、SIM 75.31%，X Voice 上 WER 20.48%、SIM 63.00%。

aihotHighlights.title: cuTile Rust：安全无数据竞争的 GPU 内核系统
aihotHighlights.summary: cuTile Rust 是一个基于 tile 的 GPU 编程系统，允许用 Rust 编写内存安全、无数据竞争的内核。它通过 `#【cutile：：module】` 宏将内核 AST 嵌入主机二进制，在运行时经 CUDA Tile IR JIT 编译为 GPU cubin。可变张量在启动前分割，不可变张量共享，启动器在 GPU 工作期间保持所有权。在 NVIDIA B200 上，逐元素操作达 7 TB/s（约 91% 峰值带宽），GEMM 达 2 PFlop/s（约 92% 密集 f16 峰值）。基于 cuTile Rust 构建的 Grout 推理引擎在 RTX 5090 上解码 Qwen3-4B 达 171 tokens/s，在 B200 上解码 Qwen3-32B 达 82 tokens/s。项目处于早期研究阶段。

aihotHighlights.title: 首个统一科学大模型 LOGOS 正式开源
aihotHighlights.summary: LOGOS 由 ATH-Token Foundry 联合中国人民大学高瓴人工智能学院开源，是首个基于统一"科学语法"的多领域科学生成基础模型。LOGOS-1B（1B参数）在六大科学任务上匹配或超越领域专用方法：口袋条件配体生成纯序列范式首次超越3D扩散模型，超越NatureLM（8×7B）；逆合成预测Top-1准确率74.8%；口袋位点识别仅靠序列达58.5% Top-n准确率；MOF材料生成NBB提升至17.78%。模型采用统一词汇表将蛋白质、小分子等编码为离散Token，通过空间交互离散化实现无需3D坐标的序列预测。预训练与下游任务形式与目标一致，跨领域知识迁移经实验验证有效。已完整开源模型权重、推理代码与技术报告。

aihotHighlights.title: 上交所发布指引：AI大模型企业可适用科创板第五套上市标准
aihotHighlights.summary: 上海证券交易所6月17日发布指引，支持尚未形成稳定收入的优质人工智能大模型企业通过科创板第五套上市标准发行上市。申报企业需在行业地位、产业链优势、目标市场需求、研发进度及关键指标方面具备突出竞争力。指引明确，申报时至少有一个大模型产品已完成上线发布并实现规模化应用，以验证商业模式可行性。下一步，上交所将在中国证监会指导下推进符合标准的企业上市。

aihotHighlights.title: 苹果 Xcode 27 核心首次深度集成 AI 智能体：支持自然语言修 Bug、构建 App
aihotHighlights.summary: 在 2026 年 WWDC 期间，苹果发布 Xcode 27，其核心组件首次整合 AI 智能体，能理解 Swift 语言并通过多轮自然语言对话辅助开发。AI 可跨多个文件修改整个代码库，也能根据提示与资源生成应用设计并独立构建完整应用，建成后仍可通过对话添加特效、动画等。Xcode 27 支持接入 Anthropic、OpenAI 和 Google 等第三方 AI 模型，同时引入 Core AI 框架提供现代 Swift API 调用端侧模型，并升级开源框架 MLX。

aihotHighlights.title: 泄露文件显示OpenAI年营收130亿但亏损远超收入
aihotHighlights.summary: OpenAI 2025年营收130.7亿美元（2024年37亿），但研发成本达191.8亿（含向微软支付105.9亿），收入成本（推理计算）75亿，销售营销成本57.3亿，运营亏损209.2亿。2025年净亏损约390亿，扣除约300亿一次性会计费用后约80亿。2025年3月获1220亿融资（估值8520亿）。ChatGPT周活超9亿，付费约5000万。为控制成本已关闭Sora视频模型并削减非核心业务。

aihotHighlights.title: 库克：AI 浪潮引发存储芯片价格暴涨，iPhone 等苹果产品涨价已"不可避免"
aihotHighlights.summary: 苹果CEO库克确认，AI热潮导致存储芯片严重短缺和价格暴涨，苹果产品涨价已"不可避免"。库克未透露涨价具体细节。华尔街日报指出，全球AI巨头大幅增加资本开支，高带宽内存需求激增，挤压消费电子芯片供应。自2024年以来内存和存储芯片价格已翻四倍，涨势预计延续至2027年。研究机构估算，下一代iPhone 18 Pro售价或需增加约270美元。苹果已在上月提高Mac Mini起售价。摩根士丹利预测，今年美国智能手机和PC价格将上涨15%。

aihotHighlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.181 发布
aihotHighlights.summary: Claude Code v2.1.181 发布，新增 `/config key=value` 语法允许在提示中直接设置任意配置项，新增 `sandbox.allowAppleEvents` 选项使沙盒命令支持 Apple Events，新增 `CLAUDE_CLIENT_PRESENCE_FILE` 环境变量用于抑制移动端推送通知。内置 Bun 运行时升级至 1.4，改进了长段落流式输出（逐行显示）和 API 连接中断后自动重试。子 agent 面板优化：空闲 agent 30 秒自动隐藏、列表最多 5 行。修复了提示缓存读取、Write/Edit 在网络驱动器产生 0 字节文件、启动性能回归（约 120ms）、启动阻塞（最长 15 秒）、macOS TUI 冻结、子 agent 时长显示错误、API 重试指示器残留、AWS 凭证刷新等问题。

aihotHighlights.title: Google 分享 A2UI 与 MCP Apps 三种集成架构模式
aihotHighlights.summary: Google 分享了三种集成 A2UI 与 MCP Apps 的架构模式，旨在结合两者优势。A2UI 采用声明式框架，通过 JSON payload 定义 UI，由宿主原生渲染，确保一致性与安全性，但受限于预定义组件库。MCP Apps 在 iframe 中使用标准 Web 技术提供自定义界面，但存在设计碎片化、性能与安全挑战。三种模式包括：通过 MCP 服务器提供 A2UI，利用 MCP Resources 或 Tool 调用传递 JSON，实现"一次编写，原生渲染"的跨平台能力；以及静态与动态交付方案。Google 正考虑扩展 MCP 以原生支持 A2UI。

raw.titleEn: A2UI + MCP Apps： Combining the best of declarative and custom agentic UIs

aihotHighlights.title: 借助 Workload Identity Federation 安全访问 Claude Platform
aihotHighlights.summary: Workload Identity Federation （WIF） 已在 Claude Platform 上全面可用。WIF 兼容任何 OIDC 身份提供者，覆盖所有 Claude API 端点（包括第一方 SDK 和 Claude Code）。WIF 用短生命期凭证替代静态 API 密钥，并引入服务账户，每个工作负载拥有独立身份、角色和审计日志。Claude Console 提供引导设置流程，支持 Admin API 进行组织管理。API 密钥可并行使用以便逐步迁移。

aihotHighlights.title: Claude Design 更新：跨项目保持品牌一致，与Claude Code协同
aihotHighlights.summary: 6月17日，Claude Design 更新，支持跨项目使用统一设计系统，并与Claude Code同步工作流。用户可直接拖拽、对齐和缩放画布元素，编辑器稳定性大幅提升。设计系统可从GitHub、设计文件或原始上传导入，团队管理员可锁定标准系统防止篡改。新增桌面端侧边栏入口及独立网页端claude.ai/design。使用限制与聊天、Claude Cowork、Claude Code共享，每次任务消耗更少token，错误率下降。支持导出PDF、PPT，集成Adobe、Canva、Gamma等工具。发布首周用户超一百万。

crossSourceHighlights.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis

items.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
items.description: A longstanding goal of research on interpretable deep learning is to replace opaque neural computations with human-meaningful symbolic descriptions. items.summary: A longstanding goal of research on interpretable deep learning is to replace opaque neural computations with human-meaningful symbolic descriptions. In this paper, we propose an approach for approximating the behavior of components of deep networks with executable programs. We focus on attention heads in transformer language models. For a given head, we first compute its associated attention matrices on a collection of randomly selected training examples. Next, we prompt a pre-trained language model with a summary of these matrices, and instruct it to generate a set of Python programs that can reproduce the associated attention patterns given only text from the input sentence. Finally, we re-rank programs according to how well our final set of programs predict behavior on held-out inputs. We demonstrate that a set of fewer than 1,000 such generated programs can reproduce the attention patterns of heads in GPT-2, TinyLlama-1.1B, and Llama-3B, achieving an average Intersection-over-Union similarity above 75% on TinyStories. Moreover, the best-fit programs can replace neural attention heads without substantially affecting model behavior: replacing 25% of attention heads with programmatic surrogates across the three models incurs only a 16% average perplexity increase, while maintaining performance on a variety of downstream question answering benchmarks. This work contributes a scalable pipeline for reverse-engineering attention heads in transformer models using human-readable, executable code, advancing a path toward symbolic transparency in neural models.

sourceItems.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
sourceItems.description: A longstanding goal of research on interpretable deep learning is to replace opaque neural computations with human-meaningful symbolic descriptions. In this paper, we propose an approach for approximating the behavior of components of deep networks with executable programs. We focus on attention heads in transformer language models. For a given head, we first compute its associated attention matrices on a collection of randomly selected training examples. Next, we prompt a pre-trained language m
sourceItems.summary: A longstanding goal of research on interpretable deep learning is to replace opaque neural computations with human-meaningful symbolic descriptions. In this paper, we propose an approach for approximating the behavior of components of deep networks with executable programs. We focus on attention heads in transformer language models. For a given head, we first compute its associated attention matrices on a collection of randomly selected training examples. Next, we prompt a pre-trained language model with a summary of these matrices, and instruct it to generate a set of Python programs that can reproduce the associated attention patterns given only text from the input sentence. Finally, we re-rank programs according to how well our final set of programs predict behavior on held-out inputs. We demonstrate that a set of fewer than 1,000 such generated programs can reproduce the attention patterns of heads in GPT-2, TinyLlama-1.1B, and Llama-3B, achieving an average Intersection-over-Union similarity above 75% on TinyStories. Moreover, the best-fit programs can replace neural attention heads without substantially affecting model behavior: replacing 25% of attention heads with programmatic surrogates across the three models incurs only a 16% average perplexity increase, while maintaining performance on a variety of downstream question answering benchmarks. This work contributes a scalable pipeline for reverse-engineering attention heads in transformer models using human-readable, executable code, advancing a path toward symbolic transparency in neural models.

items.title: MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 在 SGLang-Omni 上：原生流式 48 kHz 语音服务
items.description: MOSS-TTS-Local-Transformer-v1.5 是一款开源 TTS 模型，支持 48 kHz 立体声、零样本声音克隆、最长 10 分钟长文本合成、时长控制及 31 种语言。其核心采用 Qwen3-4B 骨干与约 2B 参数的 MOSS-Audio-Tokenizer-v2 音频编解码器，通过 12 个 RVQ 码本运行。SGLang-Omni 以三阶段流水线部署该模型。在 Seed-TTS-Eval 上词错误率 5.10%、语音相似度 69.23%，CV3-Eval 上 WER 7.48%、SIM 61.59%，MiniMax Multilingual 上 WER 6.37%、SIM 75.31%，X Voice 上 WER 20.48%、SIM 63.00%。

items.title: cuTile Rust：安全无数据竞争的 GPU 内核系统
items.description: cuTile Rust 是一个基于 tile 的 GPU 编程系统，允许用 Rust 编写内存安全、无数据竞争的内核。它通过 `#【cutile：：module】` 宏将内核 AST 嵌入主机二进制，在运行时经 CUDA Tile IR JIT 编译为 GPU cubin。可变张量在启动前分割，不可变张量共享，启动器在 GPU 工作期间保持所有权。在 NVIDIA B200 上，逐元素操作达 7 TB/s（约 91% 峰值带宽），GEMM 达 2 PFlop/s（约 92% 密集 f16 峰值）。基于 cuTile Rust 构建的 Grout 推理引擎在 RTX 5090 上解码 Qwen3-4B 达 171 tokens/s，在 B200 上解码 Qwen3-32B 达 82 tokens/s。项目处于早期研究阶段。

items.title: 首个统一科学大模型 LOGOS 正式开源
items.description: LOGOS 由 ATH-Token Foundry 联合中国人民大学高瓴人工智能学院开源，是首个基于统一"科学语法"的多领域科学生成基础模型。LOGOS-1B（1B参数）在六大科学任务上匹配或超越领域专用方法：口袋条件配体生成纯序列范式首次超越3D扩散模型，超越NatureLM（8×7B）；逆合成预测Top-1准确率74.8%；口袋位点识别仅靠序列达58.5% Top-n准确率；MOF材料生成NBB提升至17.78%。模型采用统一词汇表将蛋白质、小分子等编码为离散Token，通过空间交互离散化实现无需3D坐标的序列预测。预训练与下游任务形式与目标一致，跨领域知识迁移经实验验证有效。已完整开源模型权重、推理代码与技术报告。

items.title: 上交所发布指引：AI大模型企业可适用科创板第五套上市标准
items.description: 上海证券交易所6月17日发布指引，支持尚未形成稳定收入的优质人工智能大模型企业通过科创板第五套上市标准发行上市。申报企业需在行业地位、产业链优势、目标市场需求、研发进度及关键指标方面具备突出竞争力。指引明确，申报时至少有一个大模型产品已完成上线发布并实现规模化应用，以验证商业模式可行性。下一步，上交所将在中国证监会指导下推进符合标准的企业上市。

items.title: 苹果 Xcode 27 核心首次深度集成 AI 智能体：支持自然语言修 Bug、构建 App
items.description: 在 2026 年 WWDC 期间，苹果发布 Xcode 27，其核心组件首次整合 AI 智能体，能理解 Swift 语言并通过多轮自然语言对话辅助开发。AI 可跨多个文件修改整个代码库，也能根据提示与资源生成应用设计并独立构建完整应用，建成后仍可通过对话添加特效、动画等。Xcode 27 支持接入 Anthropic、OpenAI 和 Google 等第三方 AI 模型，同时引入 Core AI 框架提供现代 Swift API 调用端侧模型，并升级开源框架 MLX。

items.title: 泄露文件显示OpenAI年营收130亿但亏损远超收入
items.description: OpenAI 2025年营收130.7亿美元（2024年37亿），但研发成本达191.8亿（含向微软支付105.9亿），收入成本（推理计算）75亿，销售营销成本57.3亿，运营亏损209.2亿。2025年净亏损约390亿，扣除约300亿一次性会计费用后约80亿。2025年3月获1220亿融资（估值8520亿）。ChatGPT周活超9亿，付费约5000万。为控制成本已关闭Sora视频模型并削减非核心业务。

items.title: cuTile Rust：安全无数据竞争的 GPU 内核系统
items.description: cuTile Rust 是一个基于 tile 的 GPU 编程系统，允许用 Rust 编写内存安全、无数据竞争的内核。它通过 `#【cutile：：module】` 宏将内核 AST 嵌入主机二进制，在运行时经 CUDA Tile IR JIT 编译为 GPU cubin。可变张量在启动前分割，不可变张量共享，启动器在 GPU 工作期间保持所有权。在 NVIDIA B200 上，逐元素操作达 7 TB/s（约 91% 峰值带宽），GEMM 达 2 PFlop/s（约 92% 密集 f16 峰值）。基于 cuTile Rust 构建的 Grout 推理引擎在 RTX 5090 上解码 Qwen3-4B 达 171 tokens/s，在 B200 上解码 Qwen3-32B 达 82 tokens/s。项目处于早期研究阶段。

items.title: 首个统一科学大模型 LOGOS 正式开源
items.description: LOGOS 由 ATH-Token Foundry 联合中国人民大学高瓴人工智能学院开源，是首个基于统一"科学语法"的多领域科学生成基础模型。LOGOS-1B（1B参数）在六大科学任务上匹配或超越领域专用方法：口袋条件配体生成纯序列范式首次超越3D扩散模型，超越NatureLM（8×7B）；逆合成预测Top-1准确率74.8%；口袋位点识别仅靠序列达58.5% Top-n准确率；MOF材料生成NBB提升至17.78%。模型采用统一词汇表将蛋白质、小分子等编码为离散Token，通过空间交互离散化实现无需3D坐标的序列预测。预训练与下游任务形式与目标一致，跨领域知识迁移经实验验证有效。已完整开源模型权重、推理代码与技术报告。

trendEntities.summary: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.

llmSummary.whatItIs: Agent-Reach is an open-source CLI tool that enables AI agents to read and search multiple internet platforms (Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu) via MCP (Model Context Protocol) without API fees.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has gained significant star momentum (7,590 stars in 7 days, 1,380 in 24h) indicating strong developer interest in MCP-based tool calling for multi-platform data access.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: AI agent developers, MCP tool builders, developers building autonomous web-scraping agents, and product managers exploring zero-cost data ingestion pipelines.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Enables cost-effective data aggregation from multiple social and code platforms for AI agents, potentially reducing API costs and integration complexity.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides a ready-to-use MCP server for common platforms; developers can integrate into agent workflows without building custom scrapers.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Stars are attention signals, not proof of production adoption. No evidence of commercial use or long-term maintenance. Web scraping may violate platform ToS. Confidence is low due to thin evidence beyond star growth.

trendEntities.summary: Compress tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before they reach the LLM. 60-95% fewer tokens, same answers. Library, proxy, MCP server.

llmSummary.whatItIs: Headroom is an open-source tool that compresses tool outputs, logs, files, and RAG chunks before sending them to an LLM, reducing token usage by 60-95% while preserving answer quality.
llmSummary.whyNow: Rapid star growth (17k+ in 7 days) indicates strong developer interest in reducing LLM costs and latency, especially for agentic workflows and RAG pipelines.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers building LLM-powered agents, RAG systems, or cost-sensitive AI applications; platform engineers optimizing token budgets.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Directly reduces LLM API costs and latency, enabling more scalable AI features without sacrificing output quality.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Easy integration as a library, proxy, or MCP server; immediate token savings with minimal code changes.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Compression may lose nuance in edge cases; production reliability not yet proven; star growth is attention, not adoption.

trendEntities.summary: CLI proxy that reduces LLM token consumption by 60-90% on common dev commands. Single Rust binary, zero dependencies

llmSummary.whatItIs: A CLI proxy that intercepts LLM API calls to reduce token usage by 60-90% via caching, compression, and prompt optimization, implemented as a single Rust binary with zero dependencies.
llmSummary.whyNow: Sustained high star growth (2,369 stars in 7 days) and a high heat score (420) indicate strong developer interest in cost optimization for LLM usage, especially as token costs remain a pain point.
llmSummary.whoShouldCare: Developers and teams using LLM APIs heavily (e.g., for code generation, chat, or automation), platform engineers managing AI infrastructure, and product managers focused on reducing operational costs.
llmSummary.businessRelevance: Directly addresses the cost of LLM API calls, which is a significant expense for AI-powered products. A 60-90% reduction in token usage can lead to substantial savings at scale.
llmSummary.developerRelevance: Provides a drop-in solution to reduce token consumption without changing application code. Developers can integrate it as a proxy to existing LLM workflows.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Token reduction claims are based on the project's own benchmarks; independent validation is limited. The proxy may introduce latency or compatibility issues with some LLM providers. High star count reflects attention, not proven production reliability.

trendEntities.title: 泄露文件显示OpenAI年营收130亿但亏损远超收入
normalizedKeys: title:泄露文件显示openai年营收130亿但亏损远超收入
title:leaked financial docs show openai is losing billions of dollars a year

items.title: 泄露文件显示OpenAI年营收130亿但亏损远超收入
items.summary: OpenAI 2025年营收130.7亿美元（2024年37亿），但研发成本达191.8亿（含向微软支付105.9亿），收入成本（推理计算）75亿，销售营销成本57.3亿，运营亏损209.2亿。2025年净亏损约390亿，扣除约300亿一次性会计费用后约80亿。2025年3月获1220亿融资（估值8520亿）。ChatGPT周活超9亿，付费约5000万。为控制成本已关闭Sora视频模型并削减非核心业务。

items.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
items.description: A longstanding goal of research on interpretable deep learning is to replace opaque neural computations with human-meaningful symbolic descriptions. In this paper, we propose an approach for approximating the behavior of components of deep networks with executable programs. We focus on attention heads in transformer language models. items.summary: A longstanding goal of research on interpretable deep learning is to replace opaque neural computations with human-meaningful symbolic descriptions. In this paper, we propose an approach for approximating the behavior of components of deep networks with executable programs. We focus on attention heads in transformer language models. For a given head, we first compute its associated attention matrices on a collection of randomly selected training examples. Next, we prompt a pre-trained language model with a summary of these matrices, and instruct it to generate a set of Python programs that can reproduce the associated attention patterns given only text from the input sentence. Finally, we re-rank programs according to how well our final set of programs predict behavior on held-out inputs. We demonstrate that a set of fewer than 1,000 such generated programs can reproduce the attention patterns of heads in GPT-2, TinyLlama-1.1B, and Llama-3B, achieving an average Intersection-over-Union similarity above 75% on TinyStories. Moreover, the best-fit programs can replace neural attention heads without substantially affecting model behavior: replacing 25% of attention heads with programmatic surrogates across the three models incurs only a 16% average perplexity increase, while maintaining performance on a variety of downstream question answering benchmarks. This work contributes a scalable pipeline for reverse-engineering attention heads in transformer models using human-readable, executable code, advancing a path toward symbolic transparency in neural models.
items.author: Amiri Hayes, Belinda Li, Jacob Andreas
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, program synthesis, benchmark

In this paper, we propose an approach for approximating the behavior of components of deep networks with executable programs. We focus on attention heads in transformer language models. For a given head, we first compute its associated attention matrices on a collection of randomly selected training examples. Next, we prompt a pre-trained language model with a summary of these matrices, and instruct it to generate a set of Python programs that can reproduce the associated attention patterns given only text from the input sentence.
